green.text: Tell the group one thing you genuinely believe about yourself that most people get wrong.
Ask someone at this table a question you're actually curious about.
Name one person at this table you feel most comfortable around right now.
Tell the group something you've never said out loud before — keep it small.
Everyone: what's one word that describes your mood right now? Go around the table.
Say one true thing about this group that no one has said yet tonight.
Name the person at this table you've been most curious about lately.

yellow.text: Ask anyone at this table a yes/no question. They must answer out loud.
Say two true things and one lie. Don't label them. The group decides.
Point to the person you think is performing right now. Say why.
Accuse someone of avoiding a real answer tonight. They must respond.
Everyone: point to who you trust least at this table right now. No explanation.
Name one moment tonight that felt rehearsed. Who was involved?
Ask the group: 'Who here is the most different in private than in public?'
Tell someone at this table something you've noticed about them that you've never said.
Name the person who would be hardest to catch in a lie. Explain.
Who at this table do you think has been the most honest tonight? Point to them.

red.text: Tell the person to your left one thing you actually think about them. Be honest.
Call out one thing you noticed about someone's behavior tonight. Address them directly.
Say something you believe that you think no one else at this table believes.
Two players must agree on one shared truth about the group. State it out loud together.
Name the person you think has been lying tonight. One name. No softening.
Everyone stays silent for 10 seconds. Look at each other. Then the current player reacts.
Tell the group: what's the most dangerous thing you could honestly say right now?
Everyone asks the current player the same question simultaneously: 'What are you hiding?'

blue.text: Everyone says one word that describes this group right now. Go around.
Say something genuinely kind about the person who challenged you hardest tonight.
Name one thing you want to take back from tonight. You don't have to explain.
Ask the group: 'Did tonight feel real to you?' Everyone answers yes or no.
Tell the group one moment tonight that surprised you.

black.text: Final statement: complete this sentence — 'I was honest tonight because ___.' One sentence each.
Without speaking, point to the person you think played the best game tonight.
If this group had a secret, what would it be? One answer each.
Everyone: one word for how you feel right now compared to when you started.
